author: @makersmancave9725
text: Omg. Awesome. I immediately bought 3 off eBay for £30 for some DIY projects. I had previous looked at buying some aluminium honeycomb and it would have cost me £100’s for some from an industrial supplier. Honeycomb like this used in floor and roof tiles as it’s very strong and very light and has excellent thermal properties. I expect that they will have some interesting acoustic properties too, I can see these being recycled for home made CNC and 3D printer projects.

author: @PVflying
text: Smart boards are / were made by an American firm Smart Inc. They found applications in the corporate sector in training and presentation rooms, but really took off in the U.K. due to the technology for schools programme Robert mentioned. I’ve used many of them, but always felt they needed a lot of familiarity on the part of the user to make effective use of them.  Too little time spent familiarising yourself with the features and they become a hinderance rather than a benefit. For trainers who use them all day, every day, they’re great.  I would absolutely not have chosen them as being suited to a schools environment, especially considering the maintenance/ running costs of the associated video projectors which were needed.

author: @stevecummins324
text:
Is it quantum tunnelling composite? Ie resistance varies with applied  force. 

If so, and calibrated... could be *very* useful. Say for measuring torque&power mechanically using a prony brake. Lever with hole to just fit around shaft/drum etc... Slot cut from hole to edge of lever. Wing nut& Bolt that can distort&tighten hole onto shaft. Measure force needed to keep lever stationary. Multiply by length to get torque.

author: @AtlasReburdened
text: I know the sky isn't always clear there, but I can't help but feel that that aluminum honeycomb would be incredibly useful in a solar heating application. For here in Texas, I'd be tempted to section it, drill crossways through the honeycomb, feed copper tubing through, and put some high absorption paint on the aluminum plate side after stripping off the white gloss. I think clear day sunlight at sea level comes in at ~300W/m^2, and that looks to be 4-5m^2 of surface. I know regular black matte spray paint absorbs almost 90% of incident sunlight, so that looks like a ~1300W heater to me.

author: @AndyCPugh
text:
Interactive whiteboards are incredible teaching tools. Unfortunately, technology without training turns the technology into a white elephant.  TfS didn't provide for sufficient training and there is precious little time in schools, so the overall result was IWBs were seen as a failure. Where they are used well they are, however, incredible.

Robert, what brand board was that? Are there any major differences between brands?

replies.author: @dustinsmith8341
replies.text: I live in America and have seen these in college classrooms. I would check craigslist in may or june. You could contact some universities maintenance staff or something. My university stores large bulk electronics off campus at a nearby warehouse maintained by university maintenance/groundskeeping staff.
